title: murdannia crocea subsp. crocea
intro: Murdannia crocea subsp. crocea, also known as the Golden Needle, is a type of flowering plant belonging to the family Commelinaceae. Traditionally, the plant is used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which can help to soothe skin irritations and prevent infections. The health benefits of Murdannia crocea subsp. crocea include its potential to aid in wound healing, reduce fever, and alleviate symptoms of respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. In herbal preparations, the plant's roots and leaves are often used to make teas, infusions, or decoctions, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal benefits.
